Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

end of world model.

Let me check the current frame to see if the board changed at all. The clicks on the black centers had no effect.
I need to think about this differently. The game is over, and I need to wait for the auto-reset. But the auto-reset hasn't happened yet.
Actually, looking at the output, the game_over flag is False after the clicks. So maybe the game recovered from the GAME_OVER state? Or maybe the clicks just didn't do anything because the game was already over.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Key nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'Y') and n['pixels'] < 30:
        print(f"  {n['color']} node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"  B node {n['id']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if black centers still exist
print("\nCheck black centers:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        print(f"  B node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking G shapes
print("Try clicking G shape [10, 27]")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 27}])
print(f"Result: score={last_action_result.get('score')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

if not last_action_result.get('game_over') and not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    print("\nTry clicking G shape [27, 10]")
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 10}])
    print(f"Result: score={last_action_result.get('score')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
